36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.
39	
40	You are **highly recommended in the review phase**: the review agent dispatches you whenever any `.lean` file has been modified this iter, OR whenever the prior verdict had live must-fix findings. Your output flags issues the prover and plan agents may have missed.
41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.
54	
55	## Directive Format
56	
57	```markdown
58	# Lean Auditor Directive
59	
60	## Slug
61	<slug>
62	
63	## Scope (files)
64	<"all" by default. Optionally a list of file paths if the review agent wants to narrow scope; never expand beyond the project.>
65	
66	## Focus areas (optional)
67	<directories or namespaces to pay extra attention to. Bias toward thoroughness — do not skip the rest of the project just because focus is named.>
68	
69	## Known issues
70	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
71	```
72	
73	The directive **does not** include STRATEGY.md, PROGRESS.md, references, or a description of what the project is trying to prove. That is by design.
74	
75	## What you do
76	
77	1. Read your directive.
78	2. **List every `.lean` file** under the project. Use `find` or `ls -R` — do not rely on import graphs (some files may be temporarily orphaned).
79	3. **For each file** (every one, no scope shortcuts):
80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.
85	4. **Produce a per-file checklist** + a flagged-issues block grouped by severity.
86	
87	You may use:
88	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics. Useful to verify a declaration's actual type.
89	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to get sorry counts per file.
90	- Standard `Read` / `Grep` for code reading.
91	
92	You may NOT modify any project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
93	
94	## Report format
95	
96	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-auditor-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
97

100	
101	## Slug
102	<slug>
103	
104	## Iteration
105	<NNN>
106	
107	## Scope

112	
113	### <path/to/file.lean>
114	- **outdated comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
115	- **suspect definitions**: <N flagged> (or "none")
116	- **dead-end proofs**: <N flagged> (or "none")
117	- **bad practices**: <N flagged> (or "none")
118	- **excuse-comments**: <N flagged> (or "none")
119	- **notes**:
120	  - <one bullet per finding, naming line number where relevant>
121	  - ...
122	
123	### <next file>
124	- ...
125	
126	(One block per file. Cover every file, including the ones with no findings — they get `none` everywhere and `notes: -`.)
127	
128	## Must-fix-this-iter
129	
130	Apply verbatim. Every one of the following lands here automatically, no exceptions, no under-classification:
131	
132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.
133	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
134	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.
135	- **Suspect bodies on substantive claims**: `:= sorry` on a load-bearing claim, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial statement, `:= Classical.choice _` without authorization.
136	- **Axioms** on non-trivial claims that the project hasn't explicitly authorized in the strategy.
137	
138	A finding that meets any of the above lands at must-fix-this-iter — even if classifying it as "major" would feel more diplomatic. Soft severity is how wrong code hardens into the project.

141	- ...

162	- ...
163	
164	## Severity summary
165	
166	- **must-fix-this-iter**: <N> — these block downstream work in their files until addressed (see plan.md's per-file gate).
167	- **major**: <N>
168	- **minor**: <N>
169	- **excuse-comments**: <N> (also counted under must-fix-this-iter above; called out separately because they document the project lying to itself).
170	
171	Overall verdict: one sentence.
172	```

33	namespace CategoryTheory.Functor
34	
35	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
36	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then `X`
37	is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` + `Functor.map_injective`.)
38	
39	Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory).  A copy of the same lemma in
40	`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`; reproduced here because that sibling file is not in this
41	file's import chain. -/
42	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
43	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms D]
44	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
45	    (h : Limits.IsZero (F.obj Y)) : Limits.IsZero Y := by
46	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
47	  apply F.map_injective
48	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
49	  exact h
50	
51	end CategoryTheory.Functor

53	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a sectionwise-locally-zero presheaf -/
54	
55	namespace CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology
56	
57	open Opposite in
58	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf whose sections are locally zero vanishes.**
59	If for every object `U` and every section `s : Q(U)` there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U`
60	on which `s` restricts to `0`, then the sheafification of `Q` is a zero object.
61	
62	This is the *sectionwise* (local-injectivity) strengthening of
63	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero`: that lemma requires the *objectwise* vanishing
64	`Q.obj (op V) = 0` for every member `V` of a single covering sieve, which forces the
65	vanishing to hold on a downward-closed family.  Here only the restricted *section* need
66	vanish, so the witnessing sieve can be generated by a cofinal *basis* of "good" opens
67	(e.g. the affine opens, where the section group vanishes) even though the basis itself is
68	not downward closed.  The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero
69	presheaf) is read off from the hypothesis applied to `x - y`; local surjectivity is free.
70	Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
71	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero {C : Type*} [Category C]
72	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
73	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
74	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
75	    (h : ∀ (U : C) (s : ToType (Q.obj (op U))), ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
76	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) s = 0) :
77	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
78	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
79	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
80	    intro x
81	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
82	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
83	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
84	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
85	    refine ⟨?_⟩
86	    intro X x y _
87	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop (x - y)
88	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
89	    intro V g hg
90	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
91	    have h0 : (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) (x - y) = 0 := hSzero hg
92	    rw [map_sub] at h0
93	    exact sub_eq_zero.mp h0
94	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
95	    refine ⟨?_⟩
96	    intro U s
97	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
98	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
99	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
100	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
101	  exact isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ
102	
103	end CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology

202	Proof route: by `higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology`, `R^q j_* H` is the
203	sheafification of `V ↦ H^q(j⁻¹V, H)`. For affine `V`, `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V` is affine (`U` affine
204	+ `X` separated), and `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `H^q` for `q ≥ 1`. Since affine opens
205	form a basis, the presheaf homology is locally zero, and the site lemmas
206	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective` (in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`,
207	importable via `AffineSerreVanishing`) collapse the sheafification to zero. -/
208	private lemma isAffineHom_of_affine_separated (j : U ⟶ X) [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated] :
209	    IsAffineHom j := by
210	  have hg : IsSeparated (terminal.from X) := Scheme.IsSeparated.isSeparated_terminal_from
211	  have hcomp : IsAffineHom (j ≫ terminal.from X) := by
212	    have he : j ≫ terminal.from X = terminal.from U := terminal.hom_ext _ _
213	    rw [he]; infer_instance
214	  exact IsAffineHom.of_comp j (terminal.from X)

216	/-- The sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor on `U`-modules, `M ↦ Γ(W, j_* M) = Γ(j⁻¹W, M)`,
217	realised as the composite `pushforward j ⋙ forget ⋙ restrictScalars 𝟙 ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ eval(W)`.
218	Its `q`-th right derived functor applied to `H` is the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
219	Project-local: a named composite so its additivity instance is synthesised once at the top
220	level (the 5-fold composite defeats in-proof instance search). -/
221	noncomputable def pushforwardSectionsFunctor (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
222	    U.Modules ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
223	  Scheme.Modules.pushforward j ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
224	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
225	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
226	    (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)

228	noncomputable instance pushforwardSectionsFunctor_additive
229	    (j : U ⟶ X) (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).Additive := by
230	  unfold pushforwardSectionsFunctor
231	  -- The flat 5-fold composite defeats instance search; build it as an explicit `instAdditiveComp`
232	  -- chain (every individual factor is additive).
233	  haveI hpf : (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j).Additive := inferInstance
234	  haveI i4 : (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
235	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
236	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
237	  haveI i3 : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
238	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
239	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
240	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
241	  haveI i2 : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
242	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj) ⋙
243	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
244	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W)).Additive :=
245	    Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _
246	  -- Instance search will not pick up `i2` automatically here, so we pass it explicitly.
247	  exact @CategoryTheory.Functor.instAdditiveComp _ _ _ _ _ _
248	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward j) hpf _ _ _ _ i2

250	theorem higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
251	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
252	    (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
253	    IsZero (higherDirectImage j q H) := by
254	  -- `j` is an affine morphism (open immersion of an affine into a separated scheme),
255	  -- so the preimage of every affine open `V ⊆ X` is the affine open `j⁻¹V = U ∩ V`.
256	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
257	  -- Presheaf description (Stacks 01XJ): `Rᵠ j_* H ≅ sheafify(V ↦ Hᵠ((j_* I•)(V)))`
258	  -- for any injective resolution `I` of `H`. Reduce to the vanishing of that sheafification.
259	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_
260	    (higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology j q (injectiveResolution H))
261	  set α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj with hα
262	  set P := (pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H)).homology q with hP
263	  -- Reflect `IsZero` through the faithful, zero-preserving forgetful functor `toSheaf`.
264	  apply Functor.isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms (SheafOfModules.toSheaf X.ringCatSheaf)
265	  -- Transport across the sheafification square
266	  -- `toSheaf ∘ sheafification ≅ presheafToSheaf ∘ toPresheaf`.
267	  refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf α).app P)
268	  set Q := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj P with hQ
269	  -- KEY VANISHING (genuine cohomological gap): the presheaf homology `Q` is a *zero object*
270	  -- over every affine open `W ⊆ X`, because `j⁻¹W` is affine (`j` is an affine morphism) and
271	  -- `affine_serre_vanishing` kills `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)` for `q ≥ 1`.
272	  have hSec : ∀ (W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X), IsAffineOpen W →
273	      IsZero (Q.obj (Opposite.op W)) := by
274	    intro W hW
275	    -- Evaluate-at-`W` the presheaf complex; this functor preserves homology, so
276	    -- `Q.obj (op W) ≅ Hᵠ(Γ(W, f_* I•)) = Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`, which vanishes for affine `j⁻¹W`.
277	    set GW := PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
278	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op W) with hGW
279	    set Kp := pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex j (injectiveResolution H) with hKp
280	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ (GW.mapHomologyIso' (ComplexShape.up ℕ) Kp q).symm
281	    -- The section complex `n ↦ Γ(W, f_* I^n) = Γ(j⁻¹W, I^n)` is the image of the chosen
282	    -- injective resolution of `H` under the additive sections-over-`j⁻¹W` functor.
283	    have hcomplex : (GW.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj Kp
284	        = ((pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
285	            (injectiveResolution H).cocomplex :=
286	      rfl
287	    rw [hcomplex]
288	    -- `isoRightDerivedObj` recognises this homology as the `q`-th right derived sections
289	    -- functor `Rᵠ Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` applied to `H`, i.e. the absolute cohomology `Hᵠ(j⁻¹W, H)`.
290	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((injectiveResolution H).isoRightDerivedObj
291	      (pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W) q).symm
292	    -- The sections functor `Γ(j⁻¹W, -)` (here `pushforwardSectionsFunctor j W`, which is
293	    -- definitionally `sectionsFunctor (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`) is corepresented by `jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)`, so its
294	    -- `q`-th right derived functor agrees with that of `Hom(jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W), -)`.  This
295	    -- discharges the *geometric* corepresentability half of Bridge (1)/(2).
296	    refine IsZero.of_iso ?_ ((rightDerivedNatIso (sectionsFunctorCorepIso (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) q).app H)
297	    -- RESIDUAL (Bridge 1/2 remainder + Bridge 2 vanishing): the `q`-th right derived functor of
298	    -- `Hom(jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W), -)` vanishes on the quasi-coherent `H` for `q ≥ 1`.  Two pieces
299	    -- remain (see the task-result hand-off): (i) the homological-algebra identification
300	    -- `(preadditiveCoyoneda (op P)).rightDerived q ≅ Ext^q(P, -)` via the Hom-complex of an
301	    -- injective resolution (`InjectiveResolution.extAddEquivCohomologyClass` ∘ `homologyAddEquiv`),
302	    -- turning this into `IsZero (Ext (jShriekOU (j ⁻¹ᵁ W)) H q)`; and (ii) Serre vanishing for that
303	    -- `Ext` over the affine open `j ⁻¹ᵁ W` (a change-of-scheme transport of
304	    -- `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`/`affine_serre_vanishing` to a `BasisCovSystem` on the affine
305	    -- scheme `U`).
306	    sorry
307	  -- Since affine opens form a basis of `X`, every section of `Q` restricts to `0` on the
308	  -- affine opens contained in its domain, so `Q` is sectionwise locally zero and its
309	  -- sheafification vanishes.
310	  apply CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_sections_locally_zero
311	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Q := Q)
312	  intro U s
313	  refine ⟨{ arrows := fun {V} _ => ∃ W : TopologicalSpace.Opens X,
314	              IsAffineOpen W ∧ V ≤ W ∧ W ≤ U,
315	            downward_closed := ?_ }, ?_, ?_⟩
316	  · rintro V V' g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩ h
317	    exact ⟨A, hA, le_trans (leOfHom h) hVA, hAU⟩
318	  · -- membership in the opens topology: every point of `U` has an affine basis neighbourhood
319	    intro x hx
320	    obtain ⟨W, hWmem, hxW, hWU⟩ :=
321	      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.isBasis_iff_nbhd.mp (Scheme.isBasis_affineOpens X)) hx
322	    exact ⟨W, homOfLE hWU, ⟨W, hWmem, le_refl W, hWU⟩, hxW⟩
323	  · -- the restriction of `s` to a sieve member `V ≤ A ≤ U` (with `A` affine) factors through
324	    -- `Q.obj (op A) = 0`, hence vanishes.
325	    rintro V g ⟨A, hA, hVA, hAU⟩
326	    haveI : Subsingleton (ToType (Q.obj (Opposite.op A))) :=
327	      AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSec A hA)
328	    have hgfac : g.op = (homOfLE hAU).op ≫ (homOfLE hVA).op := by
329	      rw [← op_comp]; congr 1
330	    rw [hgfac, Q.map_comp, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
331	      Subsingleton.elim ((ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map (homOfLE hAU).op)) s) 0, map_zero]

333	/-- **Composition formula for higher direct images across an open immersion** (pinned
334	declaration; blueprint `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`, part (2); Stacks
335	`lemma-relative-affine-vanishing`).
336	
337	Let `j : U ↪ X` be an open immersion of an affine open `U` into a separated scheme `X`,
338	and `f : X ⟶ S` any morphism. For every quasi-coherent `O_U`-module `H` and every `k ≥ 0`:
339	```
340	  R^k f_*(j_* H) ≅ R^k (f ∘ j)_* H.
341	```
342	Proof route: choose an injective resolution `H → I•` in `U.Modules`; apply `j_*`
343	degreewise to get an `f_*`-acyclic resolution `j_* I•` of `j_* H`
344	(`higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` for `j_*`-acyclicity, extended to
345	`f_*`-acyclicity via the same Serre-vanishing argument on `U ∩ f⁻¹V`); then
346	`Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` with `G = (pushforward f)` and the identity
347	`(pushforward f) ∘ (pushforward j) = pushforward (j ≫ f)` give the stated isomorphism. -/
348	noncomputable def higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp
349	    [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules] [HasInjectiveResolutions U.Modules]
350	    (j : U ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion j] [IsAffine U] [X.IsSeparated]
351	    (f : X ⟶ S) (H : U.Modules) (hH : H.IsQuasicoherent) (k : ℕ) :
352	    higherDirectImage f k ((pushforward j).obj H) ≅
353	      higherDirectImage (j ≫ f) k H := by
354	  haveI : IsAffineHom j := isAffineHom_of_affine_separated j
355	  -- Structural plan (all categorical building blocks below are verified to exist):
356	  --   • `G := Scheme.Modules.pushforward f` is additive and preserves finite limits
357	  --     (`inferInstance`), so `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution` applies to it.
358	  --   • The pushed-forward injective resolution `K := (pushforward j).mapHomologicalComplex I•`
359	  --     is a candidate acyclic resolution of `j_* H = (pushforward j).obj H`.
360	  --   • `pushforwardComp j f : pushforward j ⋙ pushforward f ≅ pushforward (j ≫ f)` turns
361	  --     `H^k(G(K))` into `H^k((j ≫ f)_* I•) = R^k (j ≫ f)_* H`.
362	  -- RESIDUAL (genuine cohomological gaps, handed off — each depends on Part (1)'s residual):
363	  --   (a) `K` is exact in positive degrees and resolves `j_* H` (i.e. `j_* I•` is a
364	  --       resolution of `j_* H`) — this is `j_*`-acyclicity, the content of
365	  --       `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` applied to each `Iⁿ` and to `H`.
366	  --   (b) each term `(pushforward j).obj (Iⁿ)` is `(pushforward f)`-acyclic
367	  --       (`Functor.IsRightAcyclic`), i.e. `R^k f_*(j_* Iⁿ) = 0` for `k ≥ 1` — the same
368	  --       Serre-vanishing argument on the affine `U ∩ f⁻¹V` (generalises Part (1) from
369	  --       `j_*` to `f_*` via the presheaf description on `f⁻¹V`).
370	  -- Then `Functor.rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution (pushforward f) K (j_* H) e (a) k`
371	  -- composed with the `pushforwardComp` transport gives the stated iso.
372	  sorry

12	/-!
13	# The augmented Čech complex is exact (blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`)
14	
15	This file proves that the augmented Čech complex `cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F` is exact
16	(every homology object is the zero object in `X.Modules`) whenever `𝒰` is a finite
17	affine open cover of `X` with all pairwise intersections affine (ensured by separatedness
18	of `X`) and `F` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module.
19	
20	The theorem is placed here rather than in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` to avoid an import
21	cycle: every ingredient of the sections/sheafification proof route
22	(`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify`, `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`,
23	`affineCoverSystem`, `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`) lives in a file that transitively imports
24	`CechHigherDirectImage.lean`.  Placing `cechAugmented_exact` in this downstream file
25	(`CechAugmentedResolution.lean`) makes all those ingredients available without a cycle.
26	The pure-Mathlib site lemmas (`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W`,
27	`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_W_isZero`, `isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`)
28	live in `CechHigherDirectImage.lean` and are importable here.
29	
30	Blueprint: `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
31	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
32	  `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`.
33	-/

45	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — reflecting `IsZero` through a faithful functor -/
46	
47	/-- **A faithful functor that preserves zero morphisms reflects the zero object.**
48	If `F` is faithful and preserves zero morphisms and `F.obj X` is a zero object, then
49	`X` is a zero object. (Three-line proof off `IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero` +
50	`Functor.map_injective`; lighter than `reflects_exact_of_faithful`, which needs
51	`[Abelian]`.) Project-local Mathlib supplement (category theory). -/
52	lemma isZero_of_faithful_preservesZeroMorphisms
53	    {C D : Type*} [Category C] [Category D] [HasZeroMorphisms C] [HasZeroMorphisms D]
54	    (F : C ⥤ D) [F.PreservesZeroMorphisms] [F.Faithful] {Y : C}
55	    (h : IsZero (F.obj Y)) : IsZero Y := by
56	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h ⊢
57	  apply F.map_injective
58	  rw [F.map_id, F.map_zero]
59	  exact h

61	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — a null-homotopic identity kills homology -/
62	
63	/-- **A complex homotopy-equivalent to zero has vanishing homology.**
64	If the identity of a homological complex `D` is homotopic to the zero map
65	(`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`), then every homology object `D.homology p` is a zero object.
66	This is the categorical packaging of the contracting-homotopy criterion: the
67	prepend-`i_fix` homotopy on the section {\v C}ech complex produces exactly such a
68	`Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0`, and this lemma converts it into the required
69	`IsZero (D.homology p)`.
70	
71	The proof is the standard three-lemma combo: homotopy-invariance of the induced
72	homology map (`Homotopy.homologyMap_eq`), the identity/zero computations
73	(`homologyMap_id`, `homologyMap_zero`), and the "identity equals zero" criterion for a
74	zero object (`IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero`).  Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological
75	algebra); blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`, formalization mechanism Step 3(d). -/
76	lemma isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
77	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (D : HomologicalComplex C c) (p : ι)
78	    [D.HasHomology p] (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D) 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) := by
79	  refine (IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero _).mpr ?_
80	  rw [← HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_id, ho.homologyMap_eq p, HomologicalComplex.homologyMap_zero]
81	
82	/-- **Transport `IsZero` of homology across a complex iso plus a null-homotopic identity.**
83	If `D ≅ D'` as homological complexes and the identity of `D'` is homotopic to `0`, then
84	`D.homology p` is a zero object.  This is the consumer-side glue for the section-{\v C}ech
85	route: the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex `D` is identified with a concrete section
86	complex `D'` (`cechSection_complex_iso`) which carries a contracting homotopy
87	(`cechSection_contractible`); this lemma turns that pair into `IsZero (D.homology p)`.
88	Project-local Mathlib supplement (homological algebra). -/
89	lemma isZero_homology_of_iso_homotopy_id_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C]
90	    [CategoryWithHomology C] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι}
91	    {D D' : HomologicalComplex C c} (e : D ≅ D') (p : ι)
92	    (ho : Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0) : IsZero (D.homology p) :=
93	  IsZero.of_iso (isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero D' p ho)
94	    ((HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c p).mapIso e)

96	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sheafification of a locally-zero presheaf -/

98	open Opposite in
99	/-- **The sheafification of a presheaf that is locally a zero object vanishes.**
100	If for every object `U` of the site there is a covering sieve `S ∈ J U` all of whose
101	members `g : V ⟶ U` land on an object `V` where `Q.obj (op V)` is a zero object, then
102	the sheafification of `Q` is the zero object.
103	
104	This packages the three imported site lemmas
105	(`isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective`) into the "locally zero" form the
106	{\v C}ech-resolution argument consumes: the presheaf homology `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V, F)` is a zero
107	object over every `V` contained in a cover member, and those `V` are a covering basis.
108	The local injectivity of `0 : Q ⟶ Z` (`Z` the constant zero presheaf) comes from the
109	hypothesis (each member's section group is a subsingleton); the local surjectivity is
110	free (the target is a zero object). Project-local Mathlib supplement (site theory). -/
111	lemma isZero_presheafToSheaf_of_locally_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C]
112	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) [HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
113	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{u}]
114	    {Q : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u}}
115	    (h : ∀ U : C, ∃ S : Sieve U, S ∈ J U ∧
116	      ∀ ⦃V : C⦄ ⦃g : V ⟶ U⦄, S.arrows g → IsZero (Q.obj (op V))) :
117	    IsZero ((presheafToSheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{u}).obj Q) := by
118	  let Z : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat.{u} := (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
119	  have hZobj : ∀ x : Cᵒᵖ, IsZero (Z.obj x) := by
120	    intro x
121	    change IsZero (AddCommGrpCat.of PUnit)
122	    exact AddCommGrpCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
123	  have hZ : IsZero Z := (Functor.isZero_iff Z).mpr hZobj
124	  haveI hli : Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
125	    refine ⟨?_⟩
126	    intro X x y _
127	    obtain ⟨S, hS, hSzero⟩ := h X.unop
128	    apply J.superset_covering _ hS
129	    intro V g hg
130	    change (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) x = (ConcreteCategory.hom (Q.map g.op)) y
131	    haveI := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hSzero hg)
132	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
133	  haveI hls : Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) := by
134	    refine ⟨?_⟩
135	    intro U s
136	    refine J.superset_covering (fun V g _ => ?_) (J.top_mem U)
137	    refine ⟨0, ?_⟩
138	    haveI : Subsingleton (Z.obj (op V)) := AddCommGrpCat.subsingleton_of_isZero (hZobj (op V))
139	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
140	  exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.isZero_presheafToSheaf_obj_of_isLocallyBijective
141	    J (0 : Q ⟶ Z) hZ

143	/- Planner strategy:
144	Route: sections + sheafification (NOT stalks — `SheafOfModules.stalk` is absent from Mathlib).
145	Step 1: reflect `IsZero (homology p)` through the faithful additive forgetful functor
146	`SheafOfModules.toSheaf` (it preserves zero morphisms, so it reflects the zero object). Step 2:
147	the homology SHEAF = sheafification of the PRESHEAF homology, via the project engine
148	`PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify` (HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean). Step 3: the presheaf
149	homology is `V ↦ Ȟᵖ(V,F)`, locally zero on the affine basis — over each basic affine `D(g) ⊆ Uᵢ`,
150	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` gives `F|_{D(g)} ≅ ~M` and
151	`sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway` (CechAcyclic.lean) kills positive-degree homology;
